levels.name: Initial
levels.description: Ad-hoc and reactive. No formal processes. Issues addressed as they arise.

levels.name: Managed
levels.description: Basic processes exist but are inconsistently applied. Dependent on individuals.

levels.name: Measured
levels.description: Data-driven. KPIs tracked. Management uses metrics to drive decisions.

levels.name: Optimising
levels.description: Continuous improvement culture. Predictive analytics. Proactive excellence.

measures.name: ICT Governance Profile - Authority and Accountability
measures.description: All ICT Roles and Responsibilities are formally defined, owned and approved.

levels.name: Initial
levels.label: No Roles and Responsibilities defined for this capability. Ownership is absent and activities are performed ad-hoc. Nobody is accountable if something goes wrong.
levels.exit: Draft the most important Roles and Responsibilities for managing ICT risk.

levels.name: Drafted
levels.label: Roles and Responsibilities have been drafted and a named owner assigned. The document exists but has not been formally approved and is not yet consistently followed in practice.
levels.exit: Obtain formal approval of the drafted Roles and Responsibilities from the appropriate authority.

levels.name: Approved
levels.label: Roles and Responsibilities for managing ICT risk are formally approved and the owner is accountable.
levels.exit: Review approved Roles and Responsibilities considers inputs from the ICT Risk and Reporting dimensions.

levels.name: Measured
levels.label: Roles and Responsibilities are reviewed on a regular schedule. Each review considers risk findings and KPI outcomes and uses them to validate or update who is responsible and how.
levels.exit: Update Roles and Responsibilities with the results of the Risk finding or KPI gaps.

levels.name: Reviewed
levels.label: Roles and Responsibilities are continuously updated in response to Risk findings and KPI outcomes. The improvement loop is fully closed, embedded in practice and demonstrably driving better outcomes.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness to inputs from the ICT Risk and Reporting dimensions.

measures.name: ICT Risk Management Framework - Holistic Risk Coverage
measures.description: All ICT risks and controls are formally defined, owned, and included in our risk and control self assessment.

levels.name: Initial
levels.label: No risks or controls have been identified against any Role or Responsibility for this capability. We do not yet know what could go wrong if our responsibilities are not performed.
levels.exit: Draft risks describing what could go wrong if that responsibility is not performed, and controls to prevent or detect that failure.

levels.name: Drafted
levels.label: Risks and controls have been drafted against the most important Roles and Responsibilities. We know what could go wrong but have not yet formally validated whether our controls are adequate or assessed the residual risk.
levels.exit: Formally assess and approve drafted risks and controls.

levels.name: Approved
levels.label: All drafted risks and controls have been formally assessed and approved, and controls are owned.
levels.exit: Plan each approved risk and control into the risk and control self assessment (RCSA).

levels.name: In RCSA
levels.label: Risks and controls have been planned into the risk and control self assessment (RCSA). The risk profile for this capability is formally established, actively tracked and reported.
levels.exit: Assess risks and controls and record their performance.

levels.name: Reviewed
levels.label: All risks are within tolerance and the reassessment cycle is continuous and active. Risk and control findings proactively drive updates to the Governance Profile — the improvement loop is fully closed and self-sustaining.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous reassessment and demonstrate that risk findings are actively and consistently driving updates to the Governance Profile.

measures.name: ICT Reporting Profile - Accurate and Timely Insights
measures.description: Do we provide accurate and timely insights into ICT risk management performance?

levels.name: Initial
levels.label: No KPIs defined for this capability. There is no visibility of whether Roles and Responsibilities are being performed, whether controls are working, or whether outcomes are being achieved.
levels.exit: Draft at least one KPI for this capability that is directly grounded in the Roles and Responsibilities and the identified risks.

levels.name: Drafted
levels.label: KPIs have been drafted and are grounded in the Roles and Responsibilities and identified risks. They are not yet being measured consistently or reported to stakeholders.
levels.exit: Obtain formal approval of the drafted KPIs from the appropriate authority.

levels.name: Approved
levels.label: KPIs are formally approved and the owner is accountable.
levels.exit: Review approved KPIs, considering inputs from the ICT Risk dimensions.

levels.name: Measured
levels.label: KPIs are reported to management on a formal schedule. Exceptions are escalated, tracked and owned. Reporting is reliable, consistent and used to hold accountable those responsible for performance.
levels.exit: Update KPIs with the results of the Risk dimension finding or gaps.

levels.name: Reviewed
levels.label: KPI trends are analysed proactively and gaps consistently drive updates to the Governance Profile. Reporting is no longer just measurement — it is the mechanism by which continuous improvement is sustained.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous KPI review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness — that reporting outcomes are actively and consistently driving updates to Roles and Responsibilities.

riskSustainabilityLevels.name: No Visibility
riskSustainabilityLevels.description: Risks not assessed and controls untested. No assurance can be provided to executives.
riskSustainabilityLevels.exit: Formally assess at least one risk. Record a residual risk rating in Riskonnect.

riskSustainabilityLevels.name: Out of Tolerance
riskSustainabilityLevels.description: Risks assessed. Significant or Extreme residual risk remains and controls have not yet been proven effective.
riskSustainabilityLevels.exit: Begin formally assessing controls. Demonstrate at least some controls are partially or fully effective.

riskSustainabilityLevels.name: Actively Managing
riskSustainabilityLevels.description: Still out of tolerance but controls are being formally tested and gaps are being closed. Progress is visible.
riskSustainabilityLevels.exit: Bring all assessed risks to Moderate or Low residual rating through treatment and control improvement.

riskSustainabilityLevels.name: Within Tolerance — Judgment
riskSustainabilityLevels.description: Within tolerance based on expert judgment. Residual risk is Moderate or Low but no formal control testing has been completed. We believe we are safe.
riskSustainabilityLevels.exit: Formally assess at least one control as operating effectively. Move from belief to evidence.

riskSustainabilityLevels.name: Within Tolerance — Evidenced
riskSustainabilityLevels.description: Within tolerance and formally evidenced. Controls are assessed as operating effectively and residual risk is Moderate or Low. We can prove we are safe.
riskSustainabilityLevels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous control assessment and re-testing.

riskMetrics.name: Risk Coverage
riskMetrics.description: Degree to which risks covering roles and responsibilities have been formally approved

capabilities.name: ICT Asset & Configuration Management
capabilities.description: Lifecycle management of ICT hardware, software, and cloud assets from procurement through to decommissioning.

capabilities.name: ICT Disaster Recovery Management
capabilities.description: Plans, procedures and capabilities to recover ICT services following a major disruption or disaster.

capabilities.name: IT Service Availability Management
capabilities.description: Monitoring and planning of ICT resource performance and capacity to meet current and future demands.

capabilities.name: ICT Change Management
capabilities.description: Controlled management of changes to ICT services and infrastructure to minimise risk.

capabilities.name: ICT Data Management
capabilities.description: Management of data quality, classification, storage, retention, and governance to ensure data is accurate, secure, and accessible.

capabilities.name: ICT Governance Management
capabilities.description: Frameworks, policies, and oversight mechanisms that ensure ICT activities align with organisational strategy and compliance obligations.

capabilities.name: ICT Incident Management
capabilities.description: Restoration of normal service operation as quickly as possible following unplanned disruptions.

capabilities.name: ICT Major Incident Management
capabilities.description: Coordinated response to high-impact ICT incidents to minimise business disruption and enable rapid service restoration.

capabilities.name: ICT Third-Party Risk Management
capabilities.description: Assessment and ongoing management of risks introduced by third-party vendors, suppliers, and managed service providers.

capabilities.name: ICT Systems Acquisition, Development & Maintenance
capabilities.description: Governance of the secure acquisition, development, testing, and ongoing maintenance of ICT systems and applications.

capabilities.name: ICT Vulnerability Management
capabilities.description: Identification, assessment, and remediation of security vulnerabilities across ICT infrastructure.

capabilities.name: ICT Project Management
capabilities.description: Structured management of ICT projects to ensure delivery within scope, time, and budget while managing associated risks and stakeholder expectations.
